message:
chore(beads): close wa-1igc — atomic safe-restart workflow complete

Exhaustive state-machine test
(restart_post_preflight_state_machine_invariants_hold_for_all_failure_injections)
lands in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs covering all 16 combinations
of {snapshot, stop, start, restore} = {Ok, Err} — proves the five
cross-cutting invariants compose (earliest failure wins, snapshot
metadata preserved post-snapshot, stopped_mux_pids preserved on
start failure, restore failure is non-fatal, all-Ok yields Restored).

Core workflow itself (execute_restart_workflow +
execute_restart_post_preflight + mux lifecycle helpers +
restart_operation_lock + CLI + JSON/plain output formatters) was
already in place across a dozen prior slices — this test closes the
"Atomic restart state machine" proptest acceptance item from the
bead's Test Requirements section. Dependent wa-bo6f landed the
complementary fidelity roundtrip tests (identity/scrollback/layout)
in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/snapshot_e2e.rs at d86eef9d.

Benchmarks (Criterion full_restart_cycle_*) and LabRuntime DPOR
concurrency coverage remain as documented follow-ups; they are not
preconditions for the core atomic workflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
